entry.title: Register Now for Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we'll start the year with 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

Agenda:

 

👉Don't miss this opportunity and register now! 

 

Sign-Up Instructions:

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to register for the live sessions and to consume the recordings. Find out how to sign up​

  • As a customer:​ Access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition is required. Follow these steps to sign up:​
    1. Navigate to the sign-up page and click on the “sign up” button.​
    2. Authenticate yourself with your S-user ID and password.​
    3. The system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalogue. This process may take up to 2 hours.​
    4. You will receive a registration confirmation via e-mail once the registration process is complete.​
  • As a partner: Partners can access the live sessions and recordings through the SAP Partner Edge Edition.​
    ​
  • As an SAP internal: Register here for the SAP Learning Hub Internal Edition (free of charge) by clicking on "access".​

Experiencing issues? Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Live Product Expert Sessions.

Find out more about our live product expert sessions here.

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of AI in Manufacturing with Cloud ERP
entry.content:
In today's fast-changing world, industrial manufacturers of all sizes are using artificial intelligence (AI) to improve their operations and stay ahead.

The recently published Automation World article “The AI Capabilities Midsized Manufacturers Need” explains how midsized manufacturers can use AI directly embedded in their enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, like S/4HANAN Cloud Public Edition, to become more efficient, engage better with customers, and streamline their business processes.

Let’s talk about it or learn more on GROW with SAP | Industrielle Fertigung

entry.title: Key Success Factors for Transition from SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Recently, we had the opportunity to hear from several customers and partners and their experiences transitioning from SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Their stories contained valuable insights and lessons that we felt could help other customers navigate their own transitions.

Assess Current Challenges and Set Clear Objectives

Pinpoint specific pain points and ensure that these needs can be addressed in the new solution, beginning with a clear understanding and keeping these in mind encourages clarity in decision making. Scrutinise the scope items and their capabilities, in the Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) and use the Fit-to-Standard (FTS) workshops in the Explore phase to ensure those objectives are met.

Choose the Right Implementation Partner

The choice of an implementation partner can make or break the project. The partner should have a deep understanding of your business and bridges the gap between technical requirements and organisational goals. Partners also provide expertise and dedication and are vital in creating a productive, collaborative experience which contributes significantly to project’s success.

Allocate Sufficient Internal Resources

It can be tempting to allocate minimal resources, to avoid impacting the business, but this can lead to delays or higher costs, further down the line, it is often reflected in the quality of the implementation. Customers noted that transitions require committed time and effort, especially as users balance ongoing work responsibilities with project roles.

Flexible Planning and Real-Time Adaptation

Flexibility in planning is critical during a project, unforeseen issues inevitably arise and willingness to adapt helps resolve challenges without derailing the process. There is a plan when you start, but like Norman Schwarzkopf used to say, "The plan is nothing, planning is everything". Flexibility allows course adjustment however, you should retain focus on your objectives described above.

Utilise SAP provided Accelerators

Accelerators are embedded in the Activate methodology, particular attention should be given to transition specific content, particularly the migration iFlows. The iFlows map and transform many of key master and transactional objects and can lead to significant timesaving, see these blogs for more details: Data Migration Accelerator: SAP Business ByDesign to S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition and Data Migration: SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition (XML & CSV)

Making Go-Live Just Another Day

One of the customers described a principle of making go-live “just another day”, which centres around rigorous testing and change management helping to avoid stress when at system go-live.

A customer also noted that they did not consider go-live as the ultimate success milestone, instead, they mark success after the first month-end close, once they’ve run a full cycle and completed critical tasks like closing the books and submitting reports. At this point, we move into a partner led “hyper-care”, a support phase to address any post-launch issues and stabilise operations.

Final Thoughts

Every transition is a journey, and choosing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition has opened pathways to scalability. With the right partner, resources, a flexible approach, and utilising SAP’s methodologies and resources, any organization can navigate this journey successfully. We hope these shared experiences serve as helpful guidance for others considering or undergoing such a transition.

Learn More from our Customer Transition Podcasts:

Episode 103: SAP Customer Transitioning Experience: SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Episode 111: Transitioning from SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: After Go-live with Felina and Snap

entry.title: BOM批量更改 - 批量维护物料清单抬头(Scope item 64F)
entry.content:
在传统的物料清单管理功能中，标准功能仅提供对物料清单的单个增加，删除和修改。我们了解到，客户对批量更改物料清单有着强烈的需求。SAP公有云系统在新的创新功能中推出了64F这个Scope item，支持BOM工程师方便快捷地批量更改物料清单。基于这个功能，BOM工程师可以对单个或者多个BOM的抬头进行批量更改或删除，也可对单个或者多个BOM的组件进行批量添加、更改或者删除。在执行数据在数据库层面变更之前，您还可以先模拟变更，已确保变更的可执行性。

Scope item 64F主要包括三个应用：批量维护物料清单抬头，批量添加物料清单项目，批量维护物料清单项目。我们拿“批量维护物料清单抬头”举例，看一下在公有云系统中的操作步骤：

1. 在批量维护物料清单抬头 应用的初始界面，可根据需要设置您对需要修改BOM的筛选条件：

输入物料、工厂后点击 “编辑”按钮。

2. 批量维护物料清单抬头 ，可以批量修改物料清单抬头中的“删除标识”、“基本数量”、“删除标识”等字段。

在变更这些信息时，可以选择输入ECN号进行变更， 以 批量修改“基本数量”为例，我们输入修改后的基本数量后，点击“模拟”按钮。

模拟后，可以看到模拟结果，如果成功后，可以点击“执行”，选择“后台运行作业”或“立即执行”完成变更。

 

“立即执行”后，可以看到成功变更后的结果。

查看BOM，可以看到 “基本数量” 被修改成新值。

————————————————————————————————————————————

**本博文中涉及到的截图均来源于SAP内部测试环境。

entry.title: Unlock S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Why Key Users are Crucial in Moving from Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Introduction

Transitioning from SAP Business ByDesign to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is more than a technical upgrade; it requires a strategic shift and a true Cloud Mindset. This blog post draws from our key user real-world experiences and their learnings and feedback, showing how the success of this journey hinges on the drive of these critical team members.

How to Select Key Users

  • Embrace Best Practices: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is about adopting SAP’s best practices, so don’t limit your search to Businsess ByDesign experts, find individuals who understand the limitations of the Business ByDesign, the reasons for the move and are genuinely committed to embracing the Cloud Mindset and its inherent best practices.
  • Understand the “Why”: Select individuals who understand the underlying business needs not just the business process as it is implemented, they need to be able to articulate the why behind their work.
  • Strong Communicators: Key users are the bridge between the project team and end-users, they must be able to clearly explain the benefits of adopting new business processes and address any concerns.

Where are Key Users vital in transition?

  • Championing Change: Involved from the start, key users leverage their understanding of both Business ByDesign and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (see “Training and Support” below) to effectively communicate the changes and benefits to their teams. They can bridge the gap by relating new processes to familiar Business ByDesign concepts, making them powerful advocates for change.
  • Business Process Review: During the Fit-to-Standard workshops in the Explore phase, key users play a crucial role in evaluating S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition's capabilities against current Business ByDesign processes. They champion the adoption of standard S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition processes, helping determine the optimal configurations and identify any genuine gaps.
  • Training and Support: As the first line of training and support, key users leverage their dual-system experience to anticipate user questions and tailor training effectively. They are often designated as Support or Influence key users, liaising directly with SAP and need comprehensive training on the SAP Activate methodology and relevant business process content, available on SAP Learning.
  • Beyond implementation: The most valuable feedback we receive comes from key users. Their feedback and insights and along with those gathered from the Influence Request Portal, are used to improve implementation processes and help shape future product releases.

Key User involvement from the start 

We can already see the significant involvement required of the key users and consequently the demands placed upon them, this is not without a price and gives rise to several challenges.

The Challenges of Key User Involvement

  • Time Commitment & Overburdening Key Users: Key users, often already stretched due to their expertise, are now tasked with learning a new system, participating in workshops and supporting their teams. Underestimating this time commitment is a major pitfall and a common reason for project delays. They have the potential to significantly influence and optimise business processes, but being time poor can mean missed opportunities. We’ve seen several delays in go-lives directly attributable to both key user availability and resistance to change, the next point.
  • Resistance to Change: Change is inherently disruptive. Some key users may resist S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition best practice due to a perceived loss of control, especially if they were deeply involved in customising Business ByDesign in the past. They may fear that their expertise will be undermined or be wary due to negative experiences with past implementations, so this resistance needs to be addressed proactively for a successful transition.

Overcoming the Challenges

  • Address Resistance and Involve Key Users Early: Acknowledge potential resistance and address it head-on, importantly review past implementation projects, to understand the root cause of any friction and identify lessons learned. This allows you to tailor the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation to avoid repeating past mistakes., Build trust and demonstrate that you are listening to concerns, SAP’s Activate methodology recommends key-user involvement from the Discover phase onwards and this early engagement allows them to contribute to requirements gathering and process design. We had an instance where it was the customer’s Project Manager resisting key user early involvement, including them only from the fit-to-standard workshops, aiming to avoid consuming too much of their time. This backfired and this key part of the Explore phase became derailed as key users scrambled to understand the reasons scope items were selected (or not) and even why the transition itself was the best option.
  • Proper Selection and Training: Select key users with strong communication skills, a deep understanding of business needs and a willingness to embrace and evangelise change. We have also seen great success where 2-3 users are appointed per process, with one in the lead, this not only helps lighten the load, but also a valuable forum for discussion.  The key users were also selected from different but related departments, resulting in a fresh perspective, challenging existing processes and mindset and seeing some truly innovative ideas. Successful transition projects are where we see time and budget allocated to train key users using SAP Activate’s accelerators and in particular learning journeys on SAP Learning.

 Conclusion

Key users are vital to a successful transition from Business ByDesign to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. When you invest in their selection, training, and support, you're not just implementing software; you're building a team that can truly own the change.

What are your experiences with key user involvement in implementations? Share your thoughts in the comments below. And if you'd like to learn more about how our customers have successfully transitioned please see my other blog and also hear from customers themselves in this podcasts.

Please also see my other blog: Key Success Factors for Transition from SAP Business ByDesign  to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Episode 103: SAP Customer Transitioning Experience: SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Publi...

Episode 111: Transitioning from SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: After Go-...

entry.title: Datasphere THE solution to maintain the business context & considerations for outbound data movement
entry.content:
Introduction

You probably have heard before that one of the strongest muscles that SAP Datasphere brings to the table is that it leverages, respects and maintains SAP business context from S/4HANA's semantic layer.

As Business Context I refer to: Metadata, Hierarchies, Currency Conversions, Unit of Measures, Multi-Language, Security Configurations; in other words, all objects associations that give business meaning to the data.

As an example: let's picture a simple yet common use case in supply chain analytics: you would like to understand the impact of discount negotiations you have had with your vendors/business partners in your procurement processes. Connections amongst your business partners (master data) and their related purchase orders, currency rates across countries (currency conversions), where they operate and in what levels, like international, national and regional geographic-wise (hierarchies) -just to mention few examples- act as the "glue" (business context) that gives business meaning on the "raw" data.

In my previous blog, I talk about the great flexibility that SAP Datasphere offers for data integration to enable Business Data Fabric Architectures. Especially when leveraging data federation mechanisms to reduce storage costs and copies of the data. However, it is known that depending on the use case and performance on the usage of high volumes of data, data replication might be the best option.

S/4HANA plays a key role in this journey, as the ERP Cloud core system, S/4HANA combines the power of transactions and analytical engine to expose enterprise data through virtual data models that are consumed with Core Data Service (CDS) views.

Additional note: SAP S/4HANA provides over 8000 CDS views where around 20% are delta capable. These CDS views are out-of-the box available for Data Extraction, and the ones that are not, can be data-extraction enabled by creating custom CDS views which retrieve the respective associations and semantic relationships they have with other views and objects.

Check this awesome blog from @FrankRiesner for more information about released data extraction enabled CDS views per S/4HANA version.

Let's see know why Datasphere is the spot on solution to maintain the business context of your SAP Data.

 

Semantic Onboarding - Leverage your SAP Data in Datasphere

Whether your SAP data consumption is through federation or replication, the Semantic Onboarding capability is an excellent way to provide visibility to technical and business data users of all released CDS views and their relationships/associations with other objects. The way that it works is that you actually import SAP semantics to Datasphere, enabling easy and earlier access to those views for data modeling and ultimately analytics consumption.

SAP Datasphere provides mechanisms to maintain the semantics of the SAP data for many business context objects such as the ones I mentioned above: Metadata, Hierarchies, Currency Conversions, Unit of Measures, Multi-Language, Security Configurations.

The Semantic Onboarding supports different source SAP systems, such as S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA, BW/4HANA, the BW Bridge and soon calculation views from HANA Cloud will also be supported, see the roadmap item.

Let's see a quick example of how the Semantic Onboarding looks and works. Here, I'm leveraging a connection to an S/4HANA Cloud system and importing the I_Product CDS View, to later see it available as a table in the Data Builder, where data analysts can start working on data models including material master information with all of its associations: 

Semantic Onboarding: Searching I_Product CDS View for importing Material Master semantics

Semantic Onbarding: Preview of Dependent Entities of I_Product object

Data Builder: Review of I_Product Table and Associations with other views and objects pt 1

Data Builder: Review of I_Product Table and Associations with other views and objects pt 2

Data Builder: Data Lineage of I_Product on current usage in the Data Builder

Learn more about Semantic Onboarding here.

 

What about other SAP Cloud solutions? How does Datasphere leverage the Business Context of their data?

Even though this blog is focused to S/4HANA data, it is important for you to know that SAP Datasphere also provides Business Content to other SAP Cloud solutions. The Business Content are packages that includes pre-configurations to jump start analytics projects. Pre-configurations such as tables, views, facts models and analytics models, including SAP Analytics Cloud stories (prebuilt dashboards), among other data objects. These packages come out-of-the-box and you can import the ones you want in your Datasphere tenant.

Some of the packages include business content for SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba SAP Concur, SAP Signavio, SAP Success Factors, also for different Industries. Find more information here.

 

Need to Push SAP S/4HANA Data Out to a Hyperscaler Data Lake / 3rd Party Data System?

This can be a common scenario when you manage a big percentage of non-SAP data in your organization but still require certain SAP data to integrate with it. In the ever-growing data landscape, organizations may opt for 3rd party data lake solutions such as MS Azure, AWS, Google Cloud for this purpose.

In order to support customers in this journey, especially when the vast majority of data they are managing is not coming from SAP systems; SAP has launched a capability called "Premium Outbound Integration" to allow near real-time SAP data replication to 3rd party data solutions by using SAP Datasphere's Replication Flows.

SAP Datasphere's Replication Flows

*Restrictions to delta capture are listed here.

There are 2 main outbound replication options:

  1. It's inbound: therefore, I'm not explaining it further in this blog.
  2. Outbound data movement: Very handy when you want to leverage Datasphere's integration, modeling and data transformation capabilities on SAP data (and non-SAP data as well, depending on your use case), before replicating outputs to a 3rd party data system (like Azure as shown in the picture below)
  3. Pass-through option: Use Datasphere as a middleware to supported targets without any data transformation.

For more information about supported target systems, check the documentation here.

Important note: Datasphere provides the ability to replicate SAP data by leveraging ODP Data Replication APIs, leveraging Change Data Capture mechanisms from SAP systems such as S/4HANA, ECC and BW. SAP has an unpermitted usage of such ODP APIs for direct consumption in 3rd party systems, without an SAP solution such as Datasphere, 3rd party systems can consume SAP data through OData APIs, which may not be the best for consuming high volumes of SAP data. Learn more on this note.

 

Considerations of moving SAP data out

Although the Premium Outbound capability allows you to replicate SAP data out, there are some caveats you need to be aware when trying to preserve Business Context of the SAP data, being more specifically: from S/4HANA's CDS views.

Business Context like Currency Conversions, Unit of Measures, Multi-Language, Hierarchies and Security Configurations can be lost when replicated out, therefore you may need to do an extra work in your target data system to rebuild that context that has been lost.

 

  • Currency Conversions:

SAP Datasphere respects the relationships of the CDS views and its associations. SAP Datasphere applies currency conversion on the fly for local currency, company currency, global currency, etc. maintaining the business context of SAP data intact.

However, when the need is to replicate this SAP data out to a 3rd party data system (such as any of the ones mentioned above), without this enrichment you would need to select one currency of choice to export OR create a custom model for each currency converted amount in the target system. This can be cumbersome as you would need to process a higher number of measures and key figures.

For instance, when you have a dynamic nature of currency conversions (like in Datasphere) you can receive the amount on the needed currency converted amount. On the other hand, you would need to design the model with multiple columns (CAD, EUR, USD, etc). To mention a real-life scenario: when managing pricing condition tables, you'll need to multiply by the number of currencies you manage in your organization.

 

  • Unit of Measure Conversion

Similar to the Currency Conversions, SAP Datasphere has standard process to handle Unit of Measures (UoM) conversions, respecting the context coming from S/4HANA and making it easier to "translate" measures like (kg, gr, lbs, lt, gallons, etc.), especially when companies operates in multiple countries that have different measurement standards, requirements and specific company measures (like a palette of an specific product equals to 9 boxes in one country but in another equals to 12).

In the same way, when the need is to replicate this SAP data to a 3rd party data system, without the enrichment you would need to select one kind of UoM when exporting, and its business rules and context would need to be rebuilt in the 3rd party target system, potentially making it again a cumbersome process to deal with.

 

  • Multi-Language:

When leveraging SAP Datasphere for managing Analytics with SAP Data, the solution also provides the ability to pull up all the languages included in the source SAP system so that you can choose. It carries the texts of the master data in various languages. Take a look at this blog by @san_tran for an easy example.

On the other hand, when replicating the data out, you would need to select one language upfront (let's say English) and the rest of the context (texts in other languages) would need to be rebuilt in your target 3rd party data system. For this last, you would need to extract the text and the data and then separately extract every text description of your master data (for example material master) and then rejoin them again in your target 3rd party data system. This could be also cumbersome, however the option is available for use cases when it makes sense to move SAP data out to another non-SAP data system.

 

  • Hierarchies:

As SAP Datasphere leverages the Semantic Onboarding, this makes it so much easier to import semantics and associations, respecting business context of the data including, of course, hierarchies.

Similar to the previous examples, when replicating data out to a 3rd party data system, there is no accelerator to assembly hierarchies in the target system, therefore an extra and very cumbersome work would be needed to rebuilt hierarchies. Let's picture an automotive company scenario where you are dealing with millions of auto parts, multiple hierarchies including financial ones and versioning of those hierarchies. These could be expensive to maintain from scratch.

If your use case still needs to export SAP data out, take into consideration that you might need to flatten your hierarchies to become level in columns. Here users could only identify each level by their level name or number for drill down purposes (losing dynamic functionality as hierarchies might not be flexible anymore). If the wish is not to flatten hierarchies, then the rebuilt of those hierarchies would be needed to wiring them to your transaction data in the target system, this could represent higher costs as they can be multiple ones and maintenance of such hierarchies would be needed as well.

 

  • Security Configurations:

Data access controls are inherited from S/4HANA users into Datasphere. SAP BW/4HANA and SAP ECC with embedded BW authorizations can be imported into SAP Datasphere as well, making it easier to map and respect data access controls.

The approach leverages existing authorization objects from SAP S/4HANA to enforce row-level security in SAP Datasphere with Data Access Controls. Check this blog from @JensBraun to learn more about how to leverage S/4HANA authorizations into SAP Datasphere.

When replicating data out, all security configurations or data access controls get lost, leaving the unavoidable need to recreate security data access control in the 3rd party target data system.

 

Conclusions

Many organizations ask what the best solution is to manage SAP data for analytics purposes. SAP Datasphere is the spot on enterprise data warehouse solution in the cloud for this purpose. It respects and maintains the business associations of the SAP data, accelerating time to analytics with business context intact. Check my previous blog to revisit 10 reasons why SAP Datasphere makes sense for your data and analytics strategy beyond maintaining the business context of your data (like enabling a business data fabric architecture).

When managing SAP focused data use cases, the recommendation is to move forward with SAP Datasphere, leverage native connections and SAP business content. SAP Datasphere also provides multiple connectors to non-SAP sources, which means you can use Datasphere for combined enterprise-wide analytics.

Additional note: Recently, my team delivered a value case analysis based on the use of Datasphere to accelerate time to analytics while reusing and maintaining business context from S/4HANA and leveraging self-service tooling for data modeling in a Canadian Public Sector customer, resulting in a potential improvement in productivity of 50% in data modeling and in 1575 hours saved annually.

In addition, check this Gigaom field study on ROI on Datasphere, where they discovered that by using Datasphere there is an average of 72% accelerated data modeling and an approximate of 138% three-year TCO reduction vs a DIY implementation. Check the report here.

When does it make sense to replicate SAP data out? When you have other data use cases beyond SAP, or perhaps you have already invested in those non-SAP data cases with another technology, but you still want to use SAP-specific data and rebuilding the business context of the required SAP-specific data is not that cumbersome in terms of time and resource consumption in your organization.

I want to highly appreciate my data mentor, Tony Cheung, for his constant guidance on my data journey.

 

Do you want to keep learning about SAP Datasphere?

SAP continues to add innovations into Datasphere and you can check them publicly in the roadmap.
SAP also continues to grow partnerships with category leaders such as Collibra, Databricks, Google Cloud, among others, to deliver even more effective, efficient and holistic enterprise data and analytics strategies for a wide range of organizations. 

Also, take a look at these learning journeys on the SAP Learning Portal.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition赋能全景指南：2025年线上分享一览（大中华区中文版）
entry.content:
在当今快速变化的商业环境中，企业面临着如何高效利用云技术来提升运营效率和竞争力的挑战。GROW with SAP正是为了应对这一需求而推出的一套全面解决方案。它不仅整合了技术工具和服务，还提供了丰富的社区资源和学习材料，助力各种规模的企业成功采用云端ERP系统，实现智能化和可持续性的运营

GROW with SAP的核心是SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition。这是一款功能全面的云端ERP解决方案，旨在优化企业流程，提升运营效率，并推动企业迈向智能化和可持续发展的未来。

作为Cloud ERP Product Success产品成功团队的一员，我们深知帮助客户和合作伙伴充分利用SAP S/4HANA Cloud解决方案的重要性。为此，我们精心设计了多种培训和支持资源，通过专业的产品专家深入讲解每一项功能，揭示其中的价值和创新带来的优势。

让我们一起探索我们提供的不同类型的线上分享产品专家分享，这些分享已经帮助许多企业成功迈向云端智能管理的新高度。

1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 版本亮点前瞻系列博客

想要在市场变化前占得先机？这些早期发布系列分享为您提供了抢先了解最新功能和创新的机会。通过这些分享，您不仅能第一时间了解SAP S/4HANA Cloud的新特性，还能迅速应用于实际业务中，提升企业灵活应变的能力。

2. In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

如果您希望深入探讨SAP S/4HANA Cloud的应用，这个系列非常适合您。我们会详细讲解系统操作和优化方法，分享最佳实践案例，帮助您找到最适合自己企业的优化路径。这些分享让复杂的技术变得通俗易懂，使您能够轻松驾驭云端ERP系统。

3. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training

专为产品专家设计的培训分享，通过全面系统的学习，使您能够专业支持和指导客户，深入理解产品的核心价值。这不仅帮助您提升专业水平，还能够更好地为企业提供战略指导，增强企业的市场竞争力。

为了帮助您更好地了解每种分享的内容和优势，以下是一张分享概览表。如果您希望获取更多信息和定期更新，请访问相关的页面（链接如下）。

直播产品专家课程

覆盖内容

频率

entry.title: Overcoming Limitations in Public Cloud: Solving SUM of Rows in Custom CDS Views Using Eclipse ADT
entry.content:
Problem Statement: SUM of Rows in Custom CDS Views 
In On Premise System, developers have more flexibility to create complex calculations, utilizing function like Sum(). But when working with Custom CDS Views in S/4Hana Public Cloud certain operations such as summing values across multiple rows are not supported. 
 
Solution: Using Eclipse ADT to Solve the Requirement 
Let's consider an example where multiple Material Documents (Material Docs) exist for the same material, and we need to sum up the quantities for that material. 
 

Material Doc year 

Material DOC 

Material DOC Item 

Material 

Plant 

Quantity 

EntryUnit 

2024 

50000001 

1 

test 

1001 

10 

EA 

2024 

50000002 

1 

test 

1001 

50 

EA 

2024 

50000003 

1 

test 

1001 

5 

EA 

2024 

50000004 

1 

test 

1001 

100 

EA 

2024 

50000005 

1 

test 

1001 

65 

EA 

2024 

50000006 

1 

test 

1001 

40 

EA 

2024 

50000006 

2 

test 

1001 

25 

EA 

 
The requirement here is to sum the quantities for the same material (test) and plant (1001), regardless of the individual material document entries. 
we can solve the issue on ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse. 
 
Steps to Implement the Solution: 
  Create a Data Definition: In Eclipse, first, create a data definition for your custom CDS view and define view. 
 
  Here is the example code: 
define view TotalQuantiy 
as select from I_MaterialDocumentItem_2 as a  

{ 

 key a.Material,  key a.Plant, 

      a.EntryUnit as Meins, 

      a.QuantityInBaseUnit, 

      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Meins' 

      sum(a.QuantityInBaseUnit) as TotalQuantity 

} 

group by 

  a.Material, 

  a.Plant, 

  a.EntryUnit 

 
•  I_MaterialDocumentItem_2 is the CDS view that contains all the data related to individual Material Documents and quantity with material. 
•  The sum(a.QuantityInBaseUnit) is the operation,summing the QuantityInBaseUnit across multiple rows based on the material and plant. 
•  group by is used to group the records by Material, Plant, and EntryUnit to perform the sum. 
 
After defining save and activate  
 

  • The Result: 

Material 

Plant 

Total Quantity 

test 

1001 

295 

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Transforming Classic ABAP to ABAP Cloud: From Background Jobs to Application Jobs
entry.content:
Introduction: The Journey from Classic to Cloud

In the S/4HANA world, we hear about Clean Core all the time. Most of us also understand why Clean Core is such a great approach. And when we talk about S/4HANA Cloud, the Core is clean by definition.

However, I can imagine how inconvenient and counterintuitive resolving a general development task can be for a developer experienced in standard ABAP when working in the Cloud or Clean Core environment.

So, what am I sharing? In this blog, my intention is not to reiterate the principles of Clean Core, nor is it to provide detailed documentation of ABAP Cloud. Instead, I aim to show you an example of what happens when you need to solve a classic task in a new, cloud-ready way.

 

Classic ABAP: The Good, the Bad, and the Background Jobs

Any experienced ABAP developer knows what background jobs are. We use them all the time, especially when we want to run tasks periodically and automatically in the system.

When we need our custom code to execute periodically, we create a report with the required logic and schedule it as a background job step. You can refer to this blog to refresh your memory on how this is done.

There’s no doubt that even as we step into the Cloud and leave many obsolete methods behind, we still need such essential functionality. But wait - reports are not available in the Cloud!

Real life scenarios:

Imagine this: you are a seasoned SAP developer with several trusty ABAP reports for background usage that have worked perfectly for years. One day, you move to the Cloud, and you can’t take those reports with you!

Another case: you’re implementing all your developments in a Clean Core way from the beginning. You receive a requirement to code several processes and have them run autonomously in the system (e.g., a periodic data update). You know exactly how to solve it: create a report, schedule it in SM36 - but these tools are no longer available. You need a new way.

 

The Cloud Awakens: Modernising with ABAP Cloud

Good news! A new, cloud-ready method exists, and it comes in the beautiful class-based form we love in modern ABAP development.

How to do it, in a nutshell:

  1. Terminology Update: Instead of “Background Job,” you’ll now deal with an “Application Job.” The transition from SAP GUI to Fiori means the Application Jobs app is now your tool for scheduling. Learn more here.
  2. Code the Business Logic: Instead of creating a report, you’ll create a class. To make the class compatible with Application Jobs, add two interfaces: IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT and IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT. For SAP BTP ABAP Environment, different interfaces apply (see links below).
  3. Register Your Class: Register your class for Application Jobs by creating a Job Catalog Entry.
  4. Create a Job Template: To create a "variant" of your Job Catalog with prefilled fields, create a Job Template. All these steps are done in Eclipse ADT.

Once you’re finished, use the Application Jobs Fiori app to schedule a job based on the Job Template.

Profit:

Old school reports

->

Modern class-based implementation

SAP GUI: SE38, SM36, SM37

->

Eclipse native configurations & Application Jobs Fiori app

Classic development

->

Clean Core-compliant development with all its advantages

S/4HANA On Premise

->

 Any S/4HANA or BTP ABAP Environment

Let's see a real-life example.

 

Real-World Example: A Customer's Case

The problem.

One of my customers relied on a standard Analytical Query built on a complex stack of CDS views and AMDP procedures. The data selection speed was insufficient.

The solution.

We decided to persist the analytical data daily for quick access. On top of this persisted data, we created a new analytical view for high-performance data selection. To automate this process, we needed a periodic job.

Additional conditions.

The project strategy required Clean Core implementation.

Let’s see how this was achieved.

 

The How-To: Step-by-Step Guide

1. Class with business logic

Create an ABAP class for your application job:

 

Define parameters for the Application Job (e.g., using class constants)

See how this parameters will look like in the Fiori App:

We now need to define what happens, when the Application Job framework calls our class:

 
2. Job Catalog

We need to create an entry and specify the class for job execution - all in Eclipse ADT:

 
3. Job Template

Finally, we create a variant for our job. We can add values for some of the parameters:

 

Now we are all set from the development point of view. We can launch the Application Jobs app and create a job. 

 

P.S.

That’s it! The Application Jobs app includes additional features (e.g., job status monitoring) not covered in this blog. Refer to the official documentation for more.

For SAP BTP ABAP Environment, updated versions of class interfaces exist. At the time of writing, S/4HANA still uses the older versions.

This blog isn’t a comprehensive guide to all “Standard ABAP → ABAP Cloud” transformations but demonstrates how familiar concepts can be adapted for the Cloud.

I hope I’ve shown that there’s no rocket science here!

So, what’s next? Use this blog as a quick guide for ABAP Application Jobs or as inspiration for ABAP Cloud. Stay tuned for more on tackling challenges in the Classic ABAP → ABAP Cloud journey!

 

Links

  1. Application Jobs in ABAP Environment (BTP) (latest)
  2. Application Jobs in S/4HANA (latest)
  3. Application Jobs - Learning Journey

entry.title: Transportation Management Cockpit workflow tips
entry.content:
Earlier in the year, my colleague Markus Oertelt and I hosted the Product Expert session on the topic of Supply Chain – Transportation Management. As we received positive feedback on the transportation cockpit workflow tips we distilled in the session, I thought sharing with the greater community would be worthwhile.

I should point out that whilst this blog is geared toward Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. That said, it should also apply to Transportation Management stand-alone or embedded in S/4HANA.

When you are dealing with a large number of freight units to plan, whether FTL or LTL (Full vs Less than Truck Load) and have a variety of resource types (differing carrying capacity) put at your disposal by your carriers to use, it makes sense that you want to maximize the use of the available capacity of an, in order to hit a better rate and minimize the impact on the environment.

In the volume of freight units that you have to plan, you may also have a mix of FTL and LTL loads to plan.

FTL loads should be a no-brainer, as, as the name suggests, one (FTL) Freight Unit (FU) will be loaded onto one appropriate resource (I say appropriate because you of course cannot load a 20 Tonnes cargo on a resource that only has an 18 Tonnes capacity!), so here the name of the game in planning is speed – i.e you want to get these planned and out of the way and out the door quickly.

LTL loads will take more time to plan as there are opportunities to combine them to again maximize the use of a resource and get a better rate (combine several LTL loads going to the same location or plan a trip to be a single pick/multi-drop).

So how can we arrange the Transportation Planning cockpit to facilitate this task? To set the scene, let’s first see the resource types that we have available.

In our example, we are planning for ‘Road’ Transportation, and we have a variety of resources available (as offered by our carrier(s)), ranging from 1.2 to 25 tonnes in weight capacity (and 6.912 to 89.964 cubic meters in volume capacity, correspondingly). On a side note, this is something that you can also tailor in the transportation cockpit settings (i.e, the resource types presented could vary based on what you are planning. E.g, certain truck types could be available when you plan to certain regions or countries only). But I digress, the important thing to note here is that I have assigned a color coding to these resource types. For example the resource type STR012 is grey, STR075 is orange, etc….. Note that the color coding has been applied to the ‘Max Volume’ column as that is the critical capacity for me.

So how do we set these colors up?

In the ‘Trucks’ pane, click on the ‘Gear’ icon, and then define a conditional formatting, such as the one below.

All I have done is to say that if the ‘Max Volume’ is equal to ‘The volume of the resource type considered’, then assign the color ‘Colour of your choice’.

So at this point, I have a visual reference, that associates a color with a resource type.

Don’t forget to save this change to avoid having to define it every time, and set it as a default if you wish.

So you probably see where we are going here! What we want to do, is to be able to quickly identify which loads (Freight Units) can be planned onto which resource. E.g: I want loads Color-coded as Orange, to be planned to an Orange resource! So the next logical step is to add a color coding to our freight units also. However, here we have a twist, in that a load weight/volume is never constant. So whilst the condition for the trucks was ‘if volume = X cubic meters’, for the freight units, this condition will need to be something like if volume is less than / between / greater than.

To do this, we go to the ‘Freight Unit Stages’ pane, again click on the gear icon, and go to the conditional formatting tab. As an example, if we look at the volume capacity of our resource types the ‘Orange’ resource type would be appropriate for a load between 10.913 up to 35.280 Cubic meters.

 

So let’s define that as a condition.

We can define conditional formatting on multiple criteria. But let’s focus on the Gross Volume we have defined. In the smaller secondary box, you can see the gross volume condition we have defined for the gross volume to be between 10.913 and 35.280, and stated that it should adopt the color Orange (LIGHTSALMON).

Again, as this can be tedious to set up, save your changes!

If we now go back to the Planning Cockpit, we can see that our conditional formatting has been applied!

And if we look at the Freight Unit Stages and the resource types, we can see how the color coding applied, helps to quickly, and visually see what the most appropriate load/resource combinations are.

 

At this point, I could add additional filters (to reduce the list to those appropriate FU’s) or select these 4 resources and plan them. I will opt for the latter option.

I select the four Freight Units, in the Freight Unit Stages pane, and select the option to create multiple freight orders. This will result in the creation of one Freight Order per selected Freight Unit. Four freight orders have been created.

However, if we now look at the truck type assigned to each of these freight orders, we see the column is blank.

So another quick tip here, since we know that these freight units were planned with the ‘Orange’ resource type in mind, and we know that the Orange resource type is the truck type STR075, we can mass assign it to these freight orders!

To do so we select all the freight orders, and we select the mass change option.

 

In the pop-up presented, we add the resource type that we want to assign to these freight orders, in this case STR075 (note that we could also here, mass assign the carrier)

The result, as we can see is that all or selected freight orders now have the appropriate resource assigned to them

We can then save these freight orders, as the planning is complete.

I hope this blog has been instructive, and showed you one of the many ways you can tailor the transportation planning cockpit to suit and optimize your planning workflow!

entry.title: Integration of S/4HANA Public Cloud with the Online Validation Service: List of VAT Payers
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to provide a quick guide on how to setup the system integration required for the “Online Validation – List of VAT Payers” scenario for country-specific requirements in Poland. The Online Validation service is used to check the business partner tax registration number and bank account number against the List of VAT payers published by the Polish tax authorities. An overview of the scenario can be found in the official product documentation here.

Once the prerequisites mentioned in the documentation are fulfilled, here is an overview of the steps required to setup and implement the Online Validation Service:

BTP Steps

  1. Open the integration package guide for the technical setup of the Online Validation service.
  2. The first step is to download and deploy a Java Application on the BTP subaccount as described in this GitHub repository. Note that you will need the Cloud IDE Business Application Studio (BAS) to complete this step.
  3. Follow the steps explained in the GitHub documentation: the only additional step to be aware of is to change one line of code before executing the installation script. In line 4 of the install.sh file, append ‘—sso’ to the cf login command. Furthermore, in line 3, make sure to replace the example endpoint provided from the script with the real api endpoint which can be found in your BTP subaccount’s Cloud Foundry Environment overview section. In the demo subaccount used for this blog, the example endpoint from the script https://api.cf.sap.hana.ondemand.com will be replaced as shown below:
 

CPI Steps

Download the integration packages from the Integration Suite as shown here. Next, configure and deploy the necessary iFlows as described here.

Apart from the steps explained in the documentation, there are two key points to make a note of. In the iFlow “Polish List of VAT Payers Validation Service”, there is no authentication needed for the HTTP call made to the BTP Java application:

 

Secondly, in the “Route Validation Request to Online Validation Service” iFlow, it is important to change the User Role to ‘ESBMessaging.send’ in the “Configure” section.

S/4HANA Cloud Steps

At this point, the last step is the communication management setup on S/4HC side. For this, follow the steps as described here. For outbound authentication, you will need to create a service key in the VAT whitelist app binding instance on the BTP subaccount. The service key Client ID/Client secret will then be used as the outbound user credentials.

Now, the setup and configuration steps on all systems has been covered. The BTP Java application will have been deployed in a Cloud Foundry space and the CPI iFlows will have been configured and deployed as described in the documentation. Now, the final step is to execute the online validation from the S/4HC system and see the end result. For this, use the app “Online Validation for Multiple Partners”, with the company code for Poland and the Check Identification ‘PLWL’ as selection criteria:

 

Upon executing the run, the Online Validation Service will be triggered in the background and you should see the following result:

Result Code ‘1’ represent a successful validation, ‘3’ represents an invalid check result and ‘4’ signifies that the bank account details have not been maintained correctly in the business partner master data. With this, the integration between S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and the Online Validation service for the List of VAT Payers in Poland has been successfully setup and can be used to validate the compliance of business partners with the local country-specific regulations.

entry.title: Understanding Country/Region-Specific Scoping and Configuration in Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Introduction:
SAP Central Business Configuration provides flexible configuration options based on the unique requirements of each country or region, offering different sets of scenarios and activities depending on the selected scope for your project. In this blog, we will explore how selecting countries and regions impacts the available configurations, activities, and bundles. Here’s what you need to know:

  1. Scoping is country/region-specific:

    • Each country or region offers a unique set of scenarios. For example, Germany and the Philippines have different scenarios available.
    • If a scenario isn't available for a country or region, its configuration is also unavailable.

  2. Multiple countries/regions in one project:

    • You can include more than one country or region in a single project.
    • Different bundles and scenarios can be selected for each country/region.

  3. Country/region-specific activities:

    • Some configuration activities are global, meaning that they apply to all countries and regions.
    • Other activities are specific to a particular country or region, based on rules, regulations, and services offered by SAP.
    • For example:
      • Define business places configuration is available in the Philippines but not in Germany.
      • EU-specific configuration activities are not available in the Philippines.

  4. Public Sector Availability:

    • The Public Sector feature is only available for certain countries: Canada, the USA, and Germany.

By understanding how scoping and configuration works in SAP CBC, you can ensure that your project is properly configured and fulfill with business needs. Always check the specific country or region scoping and configuration before proceeding with your project setup.

entry.title: SAP CAP Lessons Learned: CSRF Token troubleshooting with S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Good day fellow CAPpers,

Today I'll share one of the lessons I've learned working with CAP, something that may not be clear in the current tutorials or documentation. It's about CSRF (Cross-site Request Forgery) errors when communicating with an S/4HANA Cloud system. Not only that, the communication was working to READ entities, but not for CREATE.

 

Prerequisites

Your SAP Business Technology Platform have is correctly configured to communicate with your S/4HANA Cloud system. The Communication Arrangement (System, Scenario, User) is all set up and it's working to READ information from the S/4HANA Cloud.

 

The Problem

After setting up all the communication, it seems that it was ready to work on BTP.

  1. The app was working locally with the mocked Business Partner imported service.
  2. The Communication was set up with S/4HANA Cloud and Destination correctly set up.
  3. I deployed the application and managed to READ/GET Business Partners.
  4. I could successfully CREATE/POST new Business Partners using Postman !!!
  5. Finally, when I tried to CREATE/POST new Business Partners on the deployed CAP application, I received a CSRF Token error. 

To debug this error, I set up a Hybrid Development, which I highly recommend when testing for development external services.

Turns out that there's a Cookie issue with the communication with S/4HANA Cloud. The CSRF token, rather than going as a header itself (x-csrf-token), it must be set inside a Cookie. I out found about this on a StackOverflow post, which led me to a journey where I finally managed to make it work.

const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const { executeHttpRequest } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client');

// ...

let filledHeaders = {};
const fetchHeader = { "x-csrf-token": "fetch" };
const result = await executeHttpRequest({
    destinationName: cds.requires.API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.credentials.destination
  },{
    method: 'GET',
    url: cds.requires.API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.credentials.path + '/A_BusinessPartner?$top=1',
    headers: fetchHeader
  });
filledHeaders['Cookie'] = result.headers["set-cookie"];
filledHeaders['x-csrf-token'] = result.headers['x-csrf-token'];
console.log(filledHeaders); // in case you want to inspect
Object.assign(req.headers, filledHeaders);

// connect to the service
const bupa = await cds.connect.to('API_BUSINESS_PARTNER');

// send the request with the custom headers
const result = await bupa.send('POST', 'A_BusinessPartner', req.data.newBupa, req.headers);
console.log(result);

Notes:

  • Keep in mind this is not a production-ready code. It can be improved (and if you know how, comment below).
  • It uses @SAP-cloud-sdk/http-client library, which is what CAP uses under the hood.
  • If you wish, it also works with Node.js https library (but not http!).
  • First you need to send a request to fetch the token. Then set it on the Cookie. Finally, send the POST with the filled headers.

 

 

May this post help you on your troubleshooting. Reply if you find any issues so I, and the community, can help.

Lucas Magrini Rigo

entry.title: How to Change Date Formats in Custom CDS Views in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Statement:
In the Custom CDS View app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there are scenarios where a report requires a column with date values to be displayed in a specific format based on business needs. However, the app does not provide a direct function to change the date format. To address this, you can leverage a calculated column and utilize predefined functions such as CONCAT and SUBSTRING. By using these functions, you can manipulate the date values into the desired format.
I will demonstrate how to achieve this using an example to address such requirements effectively.

Solution:

Scenario:
Suppose there is a column in a standard CDS view containing date values in the MM/DD/YYYY format (Figure 1.1), but you need the date to be displayed in the DD-MM-YYYY format in your report.

 


Fig 1.1

Solution:
Step 1: Create a calculative column in a custom CDS.


 




Step 2: Copy the below code and paste in the code space and click on Apply.

concat( substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7 , 2 ) , concat( '-' , concat( substring(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5 , 2 ),concat( '-' , substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1 , 4 ) ) )))



Explanation of code:

The SUBSTRING function used to extracts specific parts of the string based on the start position and length.
The CONCAT function is used to combine two or more strings into a single string.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7, 2) extracts the day (characters 7 and 2 as 7 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5, 2) extracts the month (characters 5 and 6) as 5 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1, 4) extracts the year (characters 1 through 4) as 1 is start position and 4 is the length.

The CONCAT function combines these extracted parts and inserts the '-' character as a separator between them.

Step 3: Click on Check, and once the check is complete, click on Data Browser.

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Extending Journal Content and Structure with SAP Concur Expense Data
entry.content:
Travel-to-reimburse:

Extending Journal Content and Structure with SAP Concur Expense Data (Use case: Extending the Journal Entry with the vendor description)

In today's fast-paced business environment, integrating and extending financial data across platforms is crucial for maintaining accurate and comprehensive records and extending the capability of SAP S/4HANA to meet unique business needs which can sometimes be challenging.

When SAP Financial systems receive expense reports from SAP Concur, only standard and limited fields are passed to the journal document.

To improve data tracking and for Reporting purposes, some customers need to customize and add additional Expense Data to the Journal document.  The following passages outline a process to make such enhancements possible.

This blog explains how to extend journal content and structure using the mapping of custom fields between both systems: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud and SAP Concur

 

Use Case 1: Adding vendor description to the Journal Entry Structure.

This blog post outlines one of business requirements on how to enhance the journal content and structure by incorporating Concur Expense data into SAP S/4 HANA public Cloud edition by adding the vendor description in the journal entry to offer several benefits:

  • Simplifies Reconciliation: Provides context for payments and liabilities.
  • Speeds Up Audit Process: Facilitates quicker audits by providing detailed transaction information.
  • Enhances Internal Controls: Reduces fraud risks such as duplicate payments.

 

To extend the financial Journal Entry with vendor description maintained in the expense report, follow these steps:

1. Create a Custom Field (Vendor Description) in S/4 HANA Public Cloud.

Open the “Custom Fields and Logic” App and Create a New Custom Field:

  • Click on "Create".
  • Select the Business Context relevant to where you want the custom field to be (Journal Entry).

Define the Custom Field:

  • Enter a Label and Name for the custom field.
  • Choose the Type of field (e.g., Text, Number, Date).
  • Set the Length and other properties as required.

 

Enable Field Usage:

  • In the UIs and Reports tab, enable the field for the relevant applications and reports where you want it to appear.
  • You can also enable the field for OData Services if needed.

Save and Publish:

  • Click "Save" to save your custom field.
  • Click "Publish" to make the field available in the selected business context.

 For more information about the creation of the custom field in SAP S/4 Hana Public Cloud,

see Editing Custom Fields.

2. Create a Substitution Rule:

use the "Manage Substitution and Validation Rules" app to map the custom field to the vendor description:

Open the Fiori App:

  • Navigate to the Fiori launchpad and open the "Manage Substitution and Validation Rules" app.

Create a New Rule:

  • Click on "Create Rule".
  • Enter the Business Context (Expense Report: Line Item Level).
  • Select the Event (Expense Report: Line Item Level).
  • Choose Substitution Rule as the Rule Type.
  • Click on "Create".

 Define General Information:

  • Enter a Rule Name and Description.

Set Preconditions (if any): Optional

  • Define any preconditions that must be met for the substitution to occur. This can include specific field values or conditions.

Define Substitution Details:

  • Under the Substitution section, specify the Substitute Type (Substitute with field).
  • Enter the Target Field: the field created in step 1.
  • Enter the Source Field: vendor description.

 

Save and Activate:

  • Click on "Save" to save the rule.
  • Click on "Activate" to make the rule active.

 

3. Creating an expense Report in Concur with vendor details:

This is an example of an Expense report with vendors data.

 

 4. Posting the Expense Report:

Import the document manually or waiting for the automatic import, the posting results to the bellow extended Journal entry, adding the vendor description in the structure.  

 

 Note: the vendor description should only be displayed in the Debit Line Item, and if you have the requirement to clear the vendor on the credit line item, an Implementation of custom logic (BAdI) is needed.

             

Conclusion

Integrating Concur Expense data into S/4 HANA's journal entries enhances financial transparency and control. By following the outlined steps, businesses can achieve a more detailed and accurate financial reporting system.

Stay tuned for more blog posts that will showcase additional use cases covering other business requirements needed to extend the structure and the content of the Journal Entry with SAP Concur expense data.

We always welcome feedback for our SAP Concur Integration (ICS) so if you have any topics that you think would make for an interesting and beneficial blog, then please feel free to send your blog suggestions to our team inbox: sapconcurintegartion@sap.com
