entry.title: Revolutionizing Enterprise Intelligence: SAP Business AI, Joule, and Embedded AI
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business landscape, artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a crucial driving force for innovation and growth. SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, offers a suite of AI solutions tailored to meet the diverse needs of modern businesses. Among these, SAP Business AI stands out, with two primary offerings:  Joule and Embedded AI. This blog explores the distinctions between these two powerful solutions, highlighting their unique features and capabilities.

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

What is SAP Business AI?

SAP Business AI represents a collection of intelligent technologies designed to automate and optimize business processes within the enterprise environment. By leveraging machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and other advanced AI techniques, SAP Business AI enables organizations to make data-driven decisions faster.

Picture1: Key features of SAP Business AI

Key Features of SAP Business AI

  1. Embedded AI: SAP embeds AI capabilities directly into its existing solutions, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP C/4HANA, and SAP SuccessFactors, ensuring seamless user experience and quick adoption.
  2. Automated Machine Learning: SAP Business AI leverages automated machine learning to build and train models rapidly, without the need for extensive data science expertise.
  3. Natural Language Processing (NLP): It uses NLP to enable conversational AI, allowing users to interact with SAP systems using natural language.
  4. Robotic Process Automation (RPA): SAP Business AI employs RPA to automate repetitive tasks, freeing up human resources for more strategic work.
  5. Predictive Analytics: It offers predictive analytics to help businesses anticipate future trends, customer behavior, and market changes.

This blog is going to focus on Joule and Embedded AI and how they are used in SAP S/4HANA to improve user experience and make product extensions easier and more efficient.

Understanding Joule

Joule is a platform that allows businesses to develop, deploy, and manage AI applications with ease. It serves as a comprehensive toolbox for AI and Machine Learning (ML), providing developers with the necessary resources to build intelligent solutions tailored to specific business needs. Key features of Joule include:

  1. Flexible Development: Joule offers a versatile environment for developing AI models using preferred tools and frameworks. This flexibility empowers developers to create custom AI solutions that seamlessly integrate with existing systems.
  2. Scalability: The platform supports scalable deployment of AI applications, ensuring that businesses can handle increasing volumes of data and user requests efficiently.
  3. Ecosystem Compatibility: Joule is designed to work well with various SAP and third-party applications, fostering a collaborative ecosystem where different tools and technologies can interoperate.

In the ever-evolving landscape of enterprise resource planning, SAP S/4HANA has emerged as a powerful solution, offering a wide range of functionalities to streamline business processes. However, navigating through its complex interface can sometimes be challenging for users. This is where Joule comes into play. Joule is an innovative tool designed to simplify navigation in SAP S/4HANA, enhancing user experience and productivity.

Joule is a user-friendly navigation tool that helps users quickly find and access the required transactions, reports, and other functionalities within SAP S/4HANA. It acts as a search engine within the SAP environment, making it easier for users to locate specific features without having to memorize transaction codes or navigate through multiple menus.

 Key Features of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (I don’t know if you want to include Private as well – if yes, keep it as SAP S/4HANA Cloud)

  1. Intuitive Search: Joule allows users to perform keyword-based searches. Whether you're looking for a specific transaction code, report, or even a custom SAP program, Joule can find it for you in a matter of seconds.
  2. Smart Suggestions: As you type in the search bar, Joule provides smart suggestions based on your input. This feature significantly reduces the time spent searching for the right option, enhancing overall efficiency.
  3. Personalized Experience: Joule learns from your usage patterns and adapts to your preferences. It keeps track of frequently used transactions and reports, making them easily accessible from the home screen.
  4. Integrated Navigation: Joule integrates seamlessly with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud interface, providing a unified navigation experience. This integration ensures that users can access all necessary functionalities without having to switch between different tools or interfaces.
  5. Accessibility: Joule is accessible from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud home screen, making it convenient for users to start their search right from the main interface. This eliminates the need to open separate applications or modules.

Benefits of Using Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Improved Productivity: With Joule, users can quickly find what they need, reducing the time spent on navigation. This leads to increased productivity and efficiency.
  2. Reduced Training Time: New users can easily adapt to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with Joule's intuitive interface. The tool's simplicity and user-friendliness reduce the learning curve, making training more efficient.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Joule provides a seamless and personalized experience, tailored to each user's needs. This enhances overall satisfaction and makes the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system more approachable.
  4. Better Decision Making: By making it easier to access reports and analytics, Joule helps users make informed decisions promptly. This is particularly beneficial for managers and executives who need quick access to specific data.

Steps to Use Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Access Joule: From the SAP S/4 HANA home screen, locate the Joule search bar. This is usually placed in a prominent position for easy access.
  2. Enter Keywords: Type in the keywords related to the transaction, report, or functionality you are looking for. As you type, Joule will provide relevant suggestions.
  3. Select the Desired Option: From the list of suggestions, select the desired option. Joule will navigate you directly to the relevant screen or transaction.

The screen shot below shows an example of how Joule can be used to quickly find information in your S/4HANA Cloud system. In this case the user wants to know how to create a Sales Order in the system.  Instead of having to navigate through the help menus the user can simply type “How do I create a sales order” in the Joule editor and Joule will display all the information necessary for the user to get started.

 

Figure 2: Sample of navigation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using Joule

Understanding Embedded AI

Embedded AI, as the name suggests, involves integrating AI capabilities directly into SAP's business applications and processes. Unlike Joule (Joule doesn’t include SAP per branding guidelines), which is more of a platform for development, Embedded AI focuses on delivering pre-built AI functionalities to enhance the performance and user experience of existing SAP solutions. Key aspects of Embedded AI include:

  1. Seamless Integration: Embedded AI functionalities are deeply integrated within SAP applications, providing out-of-the-box capabilities that can be utilized immediately without extensive development or configuration.
  2. Ready-to-Use Models: Embedded AI offers pre-trained machine learning models tailored to common business scenarios, such as demand forecasting, fraud detection, and customer segmentation. This saves time and resources for businesses that need quick AI solutions.
  3. Real-Time Insights: By leveraging embedded AI, businesses can gain real-time insights and predictions directly within their core SAP applications, enabling faster decision-making and agility.

In the context of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, embedded AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and predictive analytics to automate tasks, provide insights, and enhance decision-making.

Use Cases of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Predictive Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Embedded AI helps predict material shortages and automatically adjusts planning data, reducing stockouts and excess inventory.
  2. Automated Invoice Processing: AI bots can read and understand invoices, match them with purchase orders, and process payments automatically, reducing manual effort and errors.
  3. Cash Forecasting: Machine learning algorithms analyze historical data and external factors to predict future cash flow, enabling better financial planning.
  4. Sales and Demand Forecasting: Predictive analytics helps forecast sales and demand more accurately, ensuring optimal inventory levels and improving service.
  5. 5. Natural Language Search: Users can interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud using natural language, making it easier to access information and perform tasks.
  6. Extensibility: Business AI can automatically generate code to create custom fields and other forms of extensions without having to manually write code

Benefits of Embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  1. Increased Efficiency: Automates repetitive tasks, freeing up employees' time for higher-value work.
  2. Improved Decision-Making: Provides real-time insights and predictive analytics for data-driven decisions.
  3. Enhanced User Experience: Offers intuitive and conversational interfaces, making the system easier to use.
  4. Reduced Costs: Streamlines processes and prevents issues, reducing waste and errors.

Getting Started with Embedded AI in SAP S/HANA Cloud

To harness the power of embedded AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, consider the following steps:

  1. Assess Your Business Needs: Identify where AI can bring the most value to your organization.
  2. Prepare Your Data: Ensure your data is clean, relevant, and accessible.
  3. Implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Upgrade to the latest version to access embedded AI features.
  4. Train and Adapt: Continuously train your AI models and adapt your business processes to maximize benefits.

The screen shots below show an example of how Embedded AI can simplify extensibility.  In most cases customers require to add custom fields to forms like Invoices and Payment Receipts.  Before Embedded AI to add these fields, it was necessary to download the template and edit it in a 3rd party software like Adobe LiveCycle Designer. This could take several hours of work.  With Embedded AI customers can add custom fields to forms by simply typing the changes they want to make in a text editor.  In the example below the user adds the field Bank Account Number by simply typing “Add Bank Account Number above Business Style and make it left aligned” in the text editor and AI makes the changes to the form without any coding.

Figure 3: Form before the changes

 

Figure 4: Form after the changes

Conclusion

SAP Business AI offers a powerful suite of tools to help businesses achieve their digital transformation goals. Joule provides a flexible platform for easy navigation and fast data retrieval.  Joule can also be used to develop and deploy custom AI solutions, catering to the needs of developers and data scientists. In contrast, Embedded AI focuses on delivering ready-to-use AI functionalities integrated within existing SAP applications, providing immediate value to end-users.

Joule and Embedded AI can be used concurrently to simplify the usage of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system by allowing users to retrieve data and perform operations faster and more efficiently.

For organizations looking to build custom AI applications, Joule also offers unparalleled flexibility and scalability. On the other hand, Embedded AI is ideal for businesses seeking quick, out-of-the-box AI solutions to enhance their existing SAP environments.

Ultimately, both Joule and Embedded AI play crucial roles in the SAP Business AI ecosystem, empowering businesses to unlock the full potential of artificial intelligence and drive sustainable growth.

Stay tuned for more insights into the evolving world of enterprise AI and its transformative impact on modern business operations.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line – High Level Introduction
entry.content:
In this blog, I will offer a glimpse into the near future of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, which will introduce a new approach to managing special testing topics to support a public cloud deployment (SAP Labs Preview Only) using a concept called the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line.

Before we jump to the future, I would like to provide a high-level overview of some of the most common SAP tasks, such as data migration, sandbox setup, and data refresh, and how you can achieve them today with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Client copies and client refreshes, while common practices in on-premise environments (such as SAP ERP 6.0 or SAP S/4HANA), are not included in the standardized operational procedure design or SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Some customers and partners, influenced by traditional on-premise tasks, may believe they can perform client copies, either local or remote, as they do on-premise. However, they later realize this is not possible.

As explained in some of my other blogs, such as Accelerated Business Transformation – Moving from SAP ERP 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, has a different system design (e.g., multi-tenancy, SaaS, operations provided by SAP, security considerations, etc.). In the public cloud, we offer different methods to implement and operate the system compared to the traditional on-premise counterparts. This distinction is crucial to ensure system readiness for continuous innovation upgrades and to maintain high standards of operational procedures.

Data Migration: The execution of data migration in the public cloud today is outlined in SAP Activate and utilizes the SAP Data Migration Cockpit. This process is primarily carried out using the concept of delta migration. We recommend our customers first simulate the migration and then perform delta migration tests until everything is ready and verified. Thousands of customers have successfully followed this approach. For additional information, you can check the following links: https://www.sap.com/documents/2023/11/c076b9ef-967e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html  & https://www.sap.com/documents/2020/07/7a263781-a67d-0010-87a3-c30de2ffd8ff.html

There are times when customers and partners wish to perform data migration mock-up loads, where they load data and then delete (or refresh) it in test or productive systems. This allows them to conduct as many full mock-up loads as needed or to correct any data loaded incorrectly. Unfortunately, this traditional practice is not included in our standardized operational procedures, so it cannot be performed. Therefore, it is advisable to always plan migrations using delta loads with simulation in mind, as described above.

SAP Data Migration Cockpit

Sandbox:  We offer our customers the option to subscribe to a Sandbox; however, unlike traditional basis tasks, you cannot copy your development or testing system or move configuration transports to a SAP S/4HANA Public Edition - Sandbox. While we provide the ability to have a Sandbox in the public cloud, the Sandbox remains independent and aligns more with the concept of "fit to standard." This means the Sandbox becomes a reference system for SAP Best Practices that you can use to check and test. If customers want the Sandbox to more closely resemble their current development or testing system, they can configure and maintain the Sandbox, but this will require them to keep it up-to-date with their current configuration settings and data. Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition follows a fit-to-standard approach, I recommend using the Sandbox primarily as a reference system for SAP Best Practices. This will allow you to explore innovations and have an environment to simulate or test extensibility. The Sandbox also offers the advantage of allowing you to test scope item elements that might require an additional subscription, among other features. However, if you have a more complex scenario for a Sandbox, such as implementing a non-standard country using the CBC - Configuration Localization Toolkit, you may need to configure the Sandbox system accordingly.

Data Refresh: In the public cloud, we offer the Test Data Refresh (TDR) service. This service has an additional subscription cost and allows our customers to refresh production data (both master and transactional) to the test system on a predefined schedule that aligns with the public cloud maintenance schedule (including upgrades and hotfix collections). The service also provides the capability for data depersonalization, which is not possible with client copies. An additional advantage compared to a system copy is that no tedious post-refresh activities are required by the customer. Integrations continue to work as before since the refresh occurs at the database level. This service is an excellent alternative for running regression testing for upgrades or future rollouts using data that closely resembles production data. You can read more about the SAP TDR service here.

SAP Test Data Refresh – Check Your System Readiness App

Looking ahead - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line is a state-of-the-art offering that provides enhanced testing flexibility in the public cloud using a parallel project line in addition to the main project line (see figure below). We will provide more information about the Parallel Project Line when it becomes generally available in the future, which is tentatively expected—subject to change—in the first half of 2025.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line

We are planning to provide customers with one parallel project line as part of their current SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition subscription. The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition three-system landscape, parallel project line was created primarily for live customers to allow them a clear and flawless separation of activities in the "main line"—the line of their productive operations. This is especially useful during lifecycle events like upgrades, which bring new innovations to the productive environment. Customers can test and validate these innovations in a project line without impacting the main line of business operations. This includes scenarios such as the extension of new scopes or the rollout of new countries. Live customers can also use it for additional testing and validation of subsequent rollout waves or for proof of concepts or exploration. Customers who are not yet live and are working on their first implementation wave can also use it for data migration tests and ad-hoc training and testing environments. Therefore, the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel project line will provide ample flexibility to customers at any stage of their lifecycle.

To summarize, this new offering will allow us to introduce more testing flexibility into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, including but not limited to:

A) Scope extensions in the parallel project line without impacting the main line, with the option to test and validate them in the parallel project line and then merge them back to the main line if successful.

B) Rollout of new non-standard countries using the Central Business Configuration – Configuration Localization Toolkit. The parallel project line will help test the new non-standard countries without the need for a Sandbox.

C) Organizational Structure Extension: Similar to scope extensions, customers will be able to test and validate organizational structure extensions in the parallel project line and then merge them back to the main line if successful.

D) Data Migration: You will be able to perform additional mock-up data migration loads with the ability to refresh (clear) the project line.

E) Build Integration: You will be able to test integrations using the parallel project line.

F) Ad-hoc Training & Testing Environment: You will be able to create an ad-hoc training or testing environment as needed.

G) Data Refresh to Parallel Project Line: The SAP Test Data Refresh Service is planned to be offered for the project line as well.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape, parallel line configuration – Organizational Structure Extension in Central Business Configuration.

I hope this content helps you understand how we plan to approach new testing methods that previously required traditional client copies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the new SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition three-system landscape, parallel line as you prepare for your project.

I would like to thank my colleagues, Sophia Sommerrock and Nicola Pace, for their review and feedback on this blog.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by adding comments to this blog.

More Information on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, release info here
    • Product videos on our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA YouTube playlist
    • SAP S/4HANA PSCC Digital Enablement Wheel here
    • Early Release Webinar Series here
    • Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud Podcast here
    • openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA here
    • Best practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, here
    • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Community: here
    • Feature Scope Description here
    • What’s New here
    • Help Portal Product Page here

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia

entry.title: Product-Centric Pricing Harmonization Two Tier ERP
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog post will detail the steps for Pricing Master integration in a Two-Tier scenario. The condition Pricing/Discounts condition for Sales created at the HQ (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP) will be replicated to the subsidiary (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) as Purchasing Info  records/Discounts conditions.

Business Requirements

Headquarter, the tier-1 establishes the pricing structure and discount terms for their products, ensuring uniformity across all the distribution organizations. These prices and discount structure are then required to be communicated automatically and applied as purchase pricing at the subsidiary, i.e. tier-2 system, in order to eliminate need for subsequent manual corrective reconciliation or adjustments.

This solution has a better fitment for the scenario for Sales office as a subsidiary and for the Distribution ecosystem scenario  wherein prices, discounts  are managed  by the headquarter and are followed by the subsidiary and distributors who place orders with the headquarter.

Capabilities

Purchase Order (PO) at Subsidiary

At the tier-2 system sales office or a distributor creates a Purchase Order (PO)  on the tier-1 system essentially for documenting the intent to buy goods from the headquarter at the agreed-upon terms (including pricing, quantity, delivery schedules, etc.). In this scenario, the PO would reflect the net price/discounts after applying the price and discount managed by the headquarter.

Sales Order (SO) at Headquarter

The tier-1 system sets the price and offers a sales discount, seasonal discounts to the sales office or distributors at the tier-2 system. This price schema would be reflected in the Sales order created against the PO at the tier-1 system.

Note : The solution capability for pricing harmonization can be set up to work bidirectionally. Prices can be replicated from tier-2 to tier 1 system as well.

Set Up

1. Subscribe and Set up Event Mesh and create Queue in Event mesh 

https://help.sap.com/docs/subscription-billing/btp-configuration/set-up-sap-event-mesh?version=2024-12-16

2. Queue should be subscribed to Sales Pricing Condition Record Events

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/19d48293097f4a2589433856b034dfa5/71703bca89464fa0a164163979327bf4.html?version=2023.002

https://api.sap.com/event/OP_SALESPRICINGCONDITIONRECORDEVENTS/overview

3. In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition Subscribe to Maintain Event Channel Binding to the Events for Sales Pricing Condition records.

4. In SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition maintain Set up Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0294

5. Create an iFlow with AMQP Adaptor that gets triggered when message is received in Queue

6. The iFlow is custom and can be designed as per custom requirement. In this case to describe an event for create is triggered when sales pricing condition record is created in SAP S/4HANA Private cloud edition. Using this event the condition record is captured as part of event; we use this to do an API call in SAP S/4HANA Private cloud Edition to get all the pricing details. Then map this pricing details to make a post API call to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for creating Purchasing info record. Then we use the Purchase Info record number to make another API post call to create Purchasing Info Record Purch. Org. Data with pricing details.

7. Before Making Post call to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition make sure communication arrangements SAP_COM_0145 are active.

Process Overview

Conclusion

The blog post details the steps for Pricing Master integration in a Two-Tier scenario. 

 

__PRESENT

entry.title: Release Assessment and Scope Dependency for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: What is new in 2025
entry.content:
Introduction

To provide our customers of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with disruption free upgrades and zero day 1 impact, we have been tirelessly improving release assessment and scope dependency tool to meet the latest customer demands.

We have implemented several changes and enhancements in the release assessment and scope dependency tool. These modifications enable us to proactively inform our customers of all Day 1 impacts relevant to their usage.

Keep watching this space for more updates on release assessment and scope dependency coming in 2025.

Applications

With the 2502 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the what’s new viewer have introduced a new attribute called ‘Action’. This was introduced to address, which tasks that are communicated via what’s new, need to be focused/prioritized so that customers could fulfil the required actions in time. This will ensure that the urgency of the tasks are communicated appropriately.

The attribute ‘Action have the following values:

  • “Critical – Immediate Action Required” - Classification for all changes that cause a day-1 impact for the customer
  • “Action/Review Required” - Classification for all changes that improve an existing process but does not cause an immediate disruption while working with the affected functionality.
  • “Innovation” = Classification for all changes that enable a new functionality or process. Customers can decide not to use the innovation or to postpone the usage.
  • “Information” = Classification for changes with minor impact, for example, structural changes of the documentation, or changed shipment cycles.

Release assessment and scope dependency have adapted itself to accommodate these changes and the application tile have been redesigned as follows. This ensures that release assessment and scope dependency communicates the changes with appropriate urgency to our customers.

New Features

We have also consolidated the ‘New Features’ tile to include the number of features delivered in a particular release, to ease the consumption of the tool.

Conclusion

We are continuously trying to add more features to the tool so that upgrades are hassle free and our customer can derive more value out of their SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation.

You can go thru our FAQ blog if you have any questions regarding release assessment and scope dependency.
